title_original: UN experts say US-Israel attack on Iran, Lebanon violates international law
content_original: UN experts say US-Israel attack on Iran, Lebanon violates international law Twelve independent United Nations human rights experts have condemned the ongoing US and Israeli military assaults on Iran and Lebanon, calling them “flagrant violations of international law.” In a joint statement, the experts warned that the conflict risks expanding into a wider regional war, "engulfing the wider region in catastrophic armed violence and threatens to set yet another precedent of total impunity for some of the world’s strongest military powers." “[The US] and Israel should stop waging and expanding wars, and considering themselves as above international legality,” said the experts, who included Francesca Albanese, the UN Special Envoy for the occupied Palestinian territory. Other experts backing the statement included Ben Saul, Michael Fakhri and Balakrishnan Rajagopal, along with the UN Working Group on discrimination against women and girls.
title_en: UN experts state US-Israel attacks on Iran and Lebanon violate international law
summary_en: Twelve independent UN human rights experts condemned ongoing military assaults by the United States and Israel against Iran and Lebanon, labeling them flagrant violations of international law. The experts warned that these actions risk expanding the conflict into a wider regional war and criticized the involved powers for acting with impunity.

IRGC says 44th missile wave launched against Israel, US bases
content_original: IRGC says 44th missile wave launched against Israel, US bases Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said it launched the 44th wave of missile attacks as part of Operation True Promise 4. The group said the strikes targeted Israel and US military positions across the region. According to the IRGC, the barrage included Khorramshahr, Kheibar Shekan, Fatah and Emad missiles and was aimed at locations including Kiryat Shmona, Haifa and US bases, including those linked to the US Fifth Fleet.
title_en: IRGC announces 44th missile wave targeting Israel and US bases
summary_en: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) stated it launched a 44th wave of missile attacks, including Khorramshahr and Kheibar Shekan variants, targeting Israeli cities such as Haifa and Kiryat Shmona, as well as US military positions linked to the Fifth Fleet. This escalation represents a continued phase of direct Iranian military strikes against both Israeli and American assets in the region.

title_original: ⚡️ Ballistic missile seen flying above Incirlik airbase in Turkey
content_original: ⚡️ Ballistic missile seen flying above Incirlik airbase in Turkey
title_en: Ballistic missile observed flying over Incirlik Air Base in Turkey
summary_en: A ballistic missile was visually confirmed flying above the Incirlik Air Base in Turkey, a location hosting significant US military assets. This incident suggests a potential escalation in the regional conflict, raising concerns about the trajectory of Iranian or proxy-launched projectiles and the security of US forces in the area.

title_original: Israeli strikes kill six in south Lebanon, health ministry says
content_original: Israeli strikes kill six in south Lebanon, health ministry says  Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon killed at least six people, Lebanon’s Public Health Ministry reported on Thursday.  Three people were killed in the town of Ain Ebel during an Israeli bombardment, the country’s National News Agency said. Three more people were killed in the town of Barish in the Tyre district as Israeli forces intensified strikes across southern Lebanon.
title_en: Israeli strikes kill six in southern Lebanon
summary_en: Israeli forces conducted bombardment in southern Lebanon, resulting in the deaths of six civilians across the towns of Ain Ebel and Barish. This escalation of strikes in the region highlights ongoing military operations along the northern border involving Hezbollah-linked areas.

Middle East war displaces over three million inside Iran: UN
content_original: Up to 3.2 million people have been displaced inside Iran since the Middle East war erupted , the United Nations refugee agency said on Thursday. "Between 600,000 and one million Iranian households are now temporarily displaced inside Iran as a result of the ongoing conflict, according to preliminary assessments," said Ayaki Ito, who heads UNHCR's emergency support team and is the refugee response coordinator for the Middle East emergency. That represents "up to 3.2 million people", he said in a statement, warning that "this figure is likely to continue rising as hostilities persist, marking a worrying escalation in humanitarian needs ". The United States and Israel launched the first wave of attacks on 28 February in a war that has seen Iran strike targets in multiple countries around the Gulf . Ito said that most of those displaced were reportedly "fleeing from Tehran and other major urban areas towards the north of the country and rural areas to seek safety". Refugee families hosted ...
title_en: Middle East war displaces over three million inside Iran: UN
summary_en: The United Nations refugee agency reported that approximately 3.2 million people have been internally displaced within Iran due to the ongoing armed conflict between the United States and Iran. This displacement, affecting up to one million households, stems from US and Israeli military strikes on Iranian territory and is expected to increase as hostilities continue.

title_original: Coordinated Iran-Lebanon missile attack targets Israel, local media reports
content_original: Coordinated Iran-Lebanon missile attack targets Israel, local media reports Missiles were launched simultaneously toward Israel from Iran and Lebanon in a coordinated attack, Israel’s Yedioth Ahronoth newspaper reported.  The report said the barrage targeted northern Israel, triggering air-raid sirens across several communities. Israeli air-defence systems intercepted projectiles fired from Lebanon, the report said, while authorities assessed the impact of missiles launched from Iran. Emergency services said there were no immediate reports of casualties.
title_en: Coordinated Iran-Lebanon missile attack targets Israel
summary_en: Iran and Lebanon-based forces launched a coordinated missile barrage targeting northern Israel, prompting air-raid sirens and interceptions by Israeli air-defense systems. While no immediate casualties were reported, the simultaneous strikes from two fronts represent a significant escalation in the ongoing conflict involving Iran and its regional allies.

content_original: ⚡️ From the site where the missile fell in the Arab town of Zarzir, west of Nazareth. All the injured are Palestinian Arabs from within Israel.
title_en: Missile strike in Zarzir, west of Nazareth, injures Palestinian Arabs
summary_en: A missile impacted the Arab town of Zarzir, located west of Nazareth in Israel, resulting in injuries among Palestinian Arab residents. This incident highlights the ongoing spillover of hostilities into northern Israel, likely linked to the broader conflict involving Iranian-backed groups and Israeli defense operations.

Both sides dig in, as Iran war approaches two-week mark
content_original: By Parisa Hafezi and Maya Gebeily DUBAI/BEIRUT, March 13 (Reuters) - The leaders of Iran, Israel and the United States all voiced defiance and vowed to fight on as the Middle East war approached the two-week mark on Friday, killing thousands of people, disrupting the lives of millions of others and shaking financial markets.
title_en: Both sides dig in as Iran war approaches two-week mark
summary_en: Leaders of Iran, Israel, and the United States reaffirmed their commitment to continued military operations as the conflict entered its second week, resulting in thousands of casualties and significant disruption to regional stability and financial markets. The statement highlights the entrenched positions of all major belligerents with no immediate signs of de-escalation.
